edges.description: Loss of CSB, XPD, XPG, or ERCC1-XPF function abolishes the downstream TC-NER complex required for processing transcription- stalling lesions.
Without functional TC-NER, transcription-blocking lesions persist and accumulate in active genes.
Defective NER leaves UV-induced DNA damage unrepaired, producing profound cutaneous photosensitivity.
Persistent stalled transcription in proliferating neural progenitors impairs neuronal differentiation and neurogenesis.
Persistent stalled transcription in post-mitotic neurons drives progressive neurodegeneration.
Impaired prenatal neurogenesis reduces brain growth, producing congenital microcephaly.
Impaired neurogenesis contributes to cerebellar and infratentorial hypoplasia.
Impaired brain development can include prenatal ventriculomegaly.
Progressive neurodegeneration manifests as brain atrophy.
The progressive neurologic disorder includes intracranial calcifications with brain atrophy.
Neurodegeneration contributes to severe progressive psychomotor impairment.
Progressive neural degeneration can involve cochlear and retrocochlear neurons, producing sensorineural hearing loss.
Progressive neurodegeneration can involve the optic nerve, producing optic atrophy.

nodes.description: Persistent unrepaired bulky DNA adducts and oxidative lesions stall RNA polymerase II on the template strand of actively transcribed genes, preventing transcription elongation. The high transcriptional demand of neural progenitors and developing neurons makes these cells especially vulnerable.

evidence.reference_title: The role of transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair (TC-NER) during mammalian forebrain development.
evidence.snippet: Neural progenitor cells and differentiating neurons in the developing forebrain sustain high transcriptional activity and metabolic demand and are therefore vulnerable to DNA damage.

nodes.description: Progressive loss of brain parenchyma, a hallmark of the rapidly progressive neurodegeneration that defines COFS syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome with a nucleotide excision-repair defect and a mutated XPD gene, with prenatal diagnosis in a triplet pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal (COFS) syndrome is a recessively inherited rapidly progressive neurologic disorder leading to brain atrophy, with calcifications, cataracts, microcornea, optic atrophy, progressive joint contractures, and growth failure.

nodes.description: Hypoplastic cerebellum with infratentorial microscopic anomalies reminiscent of pontocerebellar hypoplasia. Frequently visible on prenatal ultrasound when COFS is detected in utero.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous ERCC5 truncating mutation in a family with prenatal arthrogryposis--further evidence of genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Cerebellar hypoplasia and ventriculomegaly were also present in two and fetal edema developed in the one fetus that survived beyond 24 weeks of gestation.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome: Report of three fetuses and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Microscopic anomalies reminiscent of pontocerebellar hypoplasia are present at the infratentorial level.

nodes.description: Intracranial calcification accompanying the progressive brain atrophy, a finding shared with Cockayne syndrome within the NER-disorder spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome with a nucleotide excision-repair defect and a mutated XPD gene, with prenatal diagnosis in a triplet pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal (COFS) syndrome is a recessively inherited rapidly progressive neurologic disorder leading to brain atrophy, with calcifications, cataracts, microcornea, optic atrophy, progressive joint contractures, and growth failure.

nodes.description: Increased skin sensitivity to ultraviolet light, reflecting the underlying NER deficiency. Severity varies with the specific NER gene defect.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: This syndrome is now recognized as a disorder belonging to the spectrum of inherited defects in Nucleotide Excision Repair (NER) resulting in profound photosensitivity.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome caused by the homozygous pathogenic variant Gly47Arg in ERCC2.
evidence.snippet: He presented with profound microcephaly, psychomotor retardation, failure to thrive, cutaneous photosensitivity, a bilateral hearing deficit and optic atrophy, thrombocytopenia, and recurrent episodes of pneumonia.

nodes.description: The TC-NER complex (CSA/CSB → UVSSA-USP7 → TFIIH-XPD → XPG → ERCC1-XPF) is impaired when any required component is missing or destabilised. Damage in actively transcribed DNA can no longer be efficiently removed. COFS shares this defect with Cockayne syndrome (see Cockayne_Syndrome.yaml); the shared biology is mirrored here rather than inherited via `conforms_to` because dismech modules are reserved for cross-disorder conserved mechanisms.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In COFS syndrome, as in Cockayne syndrome, DNA repair is impaired in the transcription-coupled NER pathway, but not in the global genome NER pathway.

evidence.reference_title: The role of transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair (TC-NER) during mammalian forebrain development.
evidence.snippet: Transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair is a specialized DNA repair pathway that helps to mitigate damage induced by 'bulky' adducts such as UV-induced pyrimidine dimers and monoadducts formed by reactive oxygen species.

nodes.description: Transcriptional arrest in proliferating neural progenitors impairs neuronal differentiation and neurogenesis, reducing neuron production. This developmental arm produces the microcephaly and cerebellar hypoplasia of COFS.

evidence.reference_title: The role of transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair (TC-NER) during mammalian forebrain development.
evidence.snippet: TC-NER safeguards genome integrity and plays an essential role in neuronal differentiation, synaptogenesis, and neurogenesis.

nodes.description: Congenital, prenatal-onset microcephaly with markedly reduced head circumference, present in fetuses and at birth.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal (COFS) syndrome is an autosomal recessive inherited disorder characterized by congenital microcephaly, congenital cataracts and/or microphthalmia, arthrogryposis, severe developmental delay, severe postnatal growth failure and facial dysmorphism with prominent nasal root and/or overhanging upper lip.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome: Report of three fetuses and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Microencephaly is present in the three fetuses with delayed development of the gyri, but normal microscopic anatomy at the supratentorial level.

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic loss-of-function variants in one of four NER genes (ERCC6/CSB, ERCC2/XPD, ERCC5/XPG, or ERCC1) eliminate or destabilise the protein product, removing its contribution to transcription-coupled NER. COFS represents the severe prenatal-onset end of the NER-deficiency allelic spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All mutations have been found in Cockayne syndrome gene, CSB, xeroderma pigmentosum genes, XPD and XPG and ERCC1 gene involved in the transcription-coupled NER pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Heterogeneity and overlaps in nucleotide excision repair disorders.
evidence.snippet: Deficiencies in NER typically result in a group of heterogeneous distinct disorders ranging from the mild UV sensitive syndrome to the cancer-prone xeroderma pigmentosum and the neurodevelopmental/progeroid conditions trichothiodystrophy, Cockayne syndrome and cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal-syndrome.

nodes.description: Optic nerve atrophy is a recognised ocular finding in COFS syndrome, reported in case series of XPD- and ERCC2-mutated patients.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome with a nucleotide excision-repair defect and a mutated XPD gene, with prenatal diagnosis in a triplet pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal (COFS) syndrome is a recessively inherited rapidly progressive neurologic disorder leading to brain atrophy, with calcifications, cataracts, microcornea, optic atrophy, progressive joint contractures, and growth failure.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome caused by the homozygous pathogenic variant Gly47Arg in ERCC2.
evidence.snippet: He presented with profound microcephaly, psychomotor retardation, failure to thrive, cutaneous photosensitivity, a bilateral hearing deficit and optic atrophy, thrombocytopenia, and recurrent episodes of pneumonia.

nodes.description: Transcriptional arrest in post-mitotic neurons drives progressive neurodegeneration of surviving neurons. This degenerative arm contributes to the severe progressive psychomotor decline of COFS, distinct from the developmental neurogenesis deficit.

evidence.reference_title: The role of transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair (TC-NER) during mammalian forebrain development.
evidence.snippet: Impaired TC-NER pathway activity manifests in tissue-level pathologies, including neurodegeneration and increased susceptibility to neurological deficits.

nodes.description: Profound bilateral sensorineural hearing loss in COFS, with histopathologic evidence of accelerated cochlear nerve degeneration (axonal loss in the spiral lamina, complete absence of afferent fibers innervating inner hair cells). Reported in ERCC2/XPD-mutated patients and confirmed at autopsy.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome as a human example for accelerated cochlear nerve degeneration.
evidence.snippet: This patient was documented as having a profound bilateral sensorineural hearing loss at the age of 2 years.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome as a human example for accelerated cochlear nerve degeneration.
evidence.snippet: Histologic evaluation revealed accelerated neural and neuronal degeneration at the cochlear and retrocochlear levels.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome caused by the homozygous pathogenic variant Gly47Arg in ERCC2.
evidence.snippet: He presented with profound microcephaly, psychomotor retardation, failure to thrive, cutaneous photosensitivity, a bilateral hearing deficit and optic atrophy, thrombocytopenia, and recurrent episodes of pneumonia.

nodes.description: Profound global developmental delay with progressive neurodegeneration typically leading to early death.

evidence.reference_title: ERCC6 founder mutation identified in Finnish patients with COFS syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal (COFS) syndrome is an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by microcephaly, congenital cataracts, facial dysmorphism, neurogenic arthrogryposis, growth failure and severe psychomotor retardation.

evidence.reference_title: First reported patient with human ERCC1 deficiency has cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome with a mild defect in nucleotide excision repair and severe developmental failure.
evidence.snippet: Patient cells showed moderate hypersensitivity to ultraviolet rays and mitomycin C, yet the clinical features were very severe and, unexpectedly, were compatible with a diagnosis of cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome.

nodes.description: Enlargement of the cerebral ventricles, detectable prenatally and reflecting the underlying neurodegeneration and impaired brain growth.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous ERCC5 truncating mutation in a family with prenatal arthrogryposis--further evidence of genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Cerebellar hypoplasia and ventriculomegaly were also present in two and fetal edema developed in the one fetus that survived beyond 24 weeks of gestation.
